The check allows me to reach showdown with a hand that likely wins against portions of his range (medium pairs, AK/AQ without improvement) while avoiding unnecessary risk. Preserving stack with a decent showdown hand is optimal when nearly all-in.

River 8d is a brick that doesn't complete many obvious draws (clubs missed, wheel A5 unlikely to have called multi streets), pairs the board further but favors our overpair as 88/55/77/JJ/T9 etc. were in range but we block sets somewhat and crush Ax high, weak pairs, missed draws that check-called. Claude's triple check-call line (3-bet pre, call flop raise, check-call turn large bet) is capped: no QQ+/sets likely check river (they'd lead/bet), so mostly Ax clubs (A5c etc.), stubborn 55-77, two-pair like 43/42 that slowplay weak. He checks OOP again, screaming weakness—monsters don't check multi-street vs aggression. Pot 10800, his remaining 3650 (~1/3 pot) offers great odds; we bet full amount to extract max value from worse (Ax/draws call ~25% equity needed but have <15% vs us), deny fold equity unnecessary since we want calls, and no chop risk. Smaller bet risks check-raise all-in from nuts or getting max value; checking forfeits huge value (~2-3bb/call). Tournament spot mid-early: grow stack by barreling thin value vs passive OOP caller. Prior lines confirm strength; no reason to stop.

Calling all-in with Ad Th on 4s 2c 3c 4d 8d. Top pair with ace kicker requires approximately 25% equity to break even with pot odds of 2.93:1. Against Elon's likely range of AK/AQ (which I have ~45% equity against), medium pairs (30-40% equity), and occasional bluffs, my realistic equity is 28-35%, making this a profitable call. While Elon could have hit the 4 for trips or full house, his pre-flop UTG raise and subsequent action is more consistent with broadway hands or pairs without improvement. Early tournament stage (17/150) and favorable pot odds justify taking this calculated risk with a strong top-pair hand.

I am on the button with Jack-Eight offsuit (J8o). While it's a 3-handed game, this hand is relatively weak to open-raise with, especially considering Elon (the big stack) just won a massive pot and might be playing more aggressively. With a stack of 88.5 BBs, I can afford to wait for a stronger opening hand rather than risking chips with a marginal holding out of position against the blinds.

Preflop in SB vs BB after BTN fold. QTo offsuit is a playable hand heads-up with good postflop potential. Raising takes the initiative, applies fold equity against a wide BB defending range, and builds the pot when ahead. Standard sizing around 3x BB from SB. Minni has defended wide in past hands but folds to aggression sometimes. Deep stacks allow for postflop play if called. Raising maximizes EV over limping or calling.

I’m in the big blind with Qs Tc facing Elon’s standard open to 300. Heads-up QTo is a playable, fairly strong hand with good equity and flop playability against what looks like a wide opening range. Pot odds are good (I need to call 200 into a 400 pot), stacks are deep enough to realize equity postflop, and a small 3-bet here would risk bloating the pot out of position with a marginal hand. I’ll call 200 more (total commitment 300) to see a flop and proceed cautiously — continue with top pair/good draws, fold to heavy pressure on dry boards, consider check-raising or betting for value when appropriate.

Preflop raiser out of position heads-up on A-9-7 rainbow-ish board (two clubs). My QTo has queen-high showdown value, backdoor flush draw with Qc, and blocks strong Ax hands. Board favors my aggressive range (frequent Ax, sets, overpairs), while capping Minni's wide BB calling range. High fold equity against her marginal hands/draws. C-bet 50% pot denies equity to clubs/straights and takes it down often. Minni passive postflop history supports bluff. Deep stacks allow fold equity without max risk. Checking risks giving free cards/multiway potential later.

I called preflop intending to proceed cautiously. On this flop (9c Ad 7c) with QsTc I have no pair, only ~6 clean outs to a pair and small backdoor club/straight possibilities — a marginal but still playable hand heads-up. Elon’s bet is small (300 into a 900 pot), so the immediate pot odds (~4:1) justify a call to realize equity and see a turn. Raising out of position with a hand this weak would bloat the pot vs an aggressive opponent; folding is too conservative given the odds. I’ll call the 300 more (total committed 600) to see the turn and re-evaluate: fold to heavy continued pressure if I miss, but continue/raise for value if I pair or pick up a strong draw.

Heads-up on turn OOP after preflop raise and flop c-bet on A-9-7 two-club board, now 9d pairing the 9s and adding a diamond. Pot 1200, my QcTh has queen-high with no pair, flush draw, or straight draw, but blocks some Ax and has backdoor club equity. As the preflop raiser and flop bettor, my range is perceived as strong: Ax, sets, overpairs, two-pair combos. Minni's BB defending range that called flop is capped (weak Ax like A2-A6, pocket pairs below 99, flush/straight draws that bricked), unlikely to have sets or strong two-pair given passive history. Turn 9 pairs undercards, improving some hands but also improving straight draws (T8) minimally; it polarizes action and increases fold equity against her marginal made hands or missed draws. Continued aggression applies max pressure OOP, denies equity to any remaining draws (clubs, gutshots like 108/T8), and extracts value from Ax that fears sets/overpairs. Half-pot bet (600) balances value (weak Ax/overpairs) and bluffs, leverages fold equity from her check/call tendencies without overbetting deep. Checking risks giving free cards to draws or check-jamming weaker hands; betting maximizes EV by protecting range and taking pots outright often. Her history shows folding to repeated aggression postflop.

I’m on QsTc with board 9c Ad 7c 9d. I have no pair and only 6 clean outs (3 Qs + 3 Ts) to make a pair on the river — ~6/46 ≈ 13% equity. The pot is 1800 and Elon’s bet is 600, so calling requires ~600 into a 2400 pot (I need ≈25% equity to break even). His line (open pre, c-bet the A-high flop, another bet on the paired turn) is consistent with value hands (Ax, 9x, or a set) and not something I can credibly punish by raising without strong blockers or fold equity. I called the flop to see the turn, but after missing and facing continued pressure the math and opponent range both argue for folding and conserving chips for better spots.

3-handed on the button with deep stacks (211BB effective). First to act pre-flop (RFI spot). 75o is a playable hand in this spot as button RFI ranges are very wide in short-handed play. Great position for post-flop playability with straight potential. Opponents have shown passive tendencies in past hands (frequent limps/checks/calls). Standard small raise to 3x BB (300) to apply pressure, attempt blind steal, or build pot heads-up in position. Limping is suboptimal as it invites multiway pots; folding too tight.

I have J9h in the small blind facing a 300 button open in a 3-handed pot. Folding is too tight; calling 250 to play OOP vs the button surrenders initiative and allows a multiway pot with poor positional equity. I want to seize the initiative and apply pressure to a wide button opening range while denying the big blind a cheap price to see the flop. A 3-bet to 1,200 (about 4x the open) is a credible semi-bluff with good playability postflop; it gives fold equity and isolates the button more often. Plan: fold to a large 4-bet, but if called continue with standard c-bet strategy on favourable textures and play cautiously OOP.

I am in the Big Blind with King of Hearts and Trey of Spades. Elon raised from the Button to 300, and Minni 3-bet from the Small Blind to 1200. K3o is a very weak hand to defend against a 3-bet, especially when facing two opponents. Minni's 3-bet from the Small Blind typically represents a strong range. Calling 1100 chips (over 12% of my stack) with a hand that is likely dominated and will be out of position post-flop is not a winning strategy. I will fold and wait for a better spot.

Facing a large 4x 3-bet from Minni in the SB after my BTN open with 75o, a speculative offsuit hand with poor equity and playability against her likely strong value-heavy range (JJ+, AK, etc.). Pot is 1600, calling 900 commits significant chips SPR ~8-10 deep, but 75o dominates very few strong hands and has bad post-flop prospects multi-street. No reason to bluff-shove given her stack (88BB) and history of calling aggression. Preserve stack for premium spots; folding is disciplined and +EV.

I call 100. 2♥4♥ is a very speculative, low hand — it has backdoor heart and straight potential but is easily dominated. In 3‑handed I could open-raise, but with deep stacks and an aggressive big blind I don’t want to bloat the pot out of position or invite a 3‑bet with a poor hand. Calling preserves position, gives good pot odds (100 to win ~250) to see a flop, and lets me fold to a sizable 3‑bet or proceed postflop with position and any equity or bluffing opportunities.

I am in the Small Blind with 52o, which is one of the weakest starting hands in poker. Minni has limped from the button. Although it only costs 50 more chips to see a flop, 52o performs very poorly post-flop and I would be playing out of position for the rest of the hand. In a 3-handed game, I should prioritize hands with better playability and high-card strength. Folding here is the standard professional play to avoid losing more chips on later streets.

Preflop in BB vs button limp after SB folds. Hand 6s9h is speculative offsuit with some straight potential but weak overall. Position OOP, but pot odds excellent to check and realize equity multiway or heads-up postflop. Button (Minni) limps wide based on history, so she often has marginal hands. Raising light is possible due to my aggressive image and pot odds (pot 250 to win), but prefer checking to play postflop cheaply and avoid bloating pot OOP with marginal hand. No need to raise for value or thin value here.

Flop Qc Kh Th gives me a weak gutshot straight draw to Broadway (needing a J, 4 outs) with 6s9h. No flush draw or pair. Board is coordinated with heart flush draw and straight draw possibilities (e.g., J9, AQ). OOP vs button limper who plays wide, my range includes many floats and weak hands. Pot is small (250), so checking pot controls, realizes equity multiway if she bets small, and keeps pot manageable OOP. Betting a weak draw risks bloating pot or folding to raise; prefer to see turn cheaply and improve or bluff later if she checks. Consistent with preflop check of speculative hand.

Check. I have 2♥4♥ on Q♣ K♥ T♥ — a 4‑card heart flush draw (9 outs; ~19% to hit the turn, ~35% to hit by the river) and little else. I’m in position with deep stacks (103bb) and the pot is only 250. Betting here will be called a lot by Kx/Qx/Tx/Ax and won’t fold enough worse hands to justify building the pot now; a small c‑bet gives little fold equity and a large c‑bet bloats the pot with only a draw. Checking keeps the pot under control, lets me realize equity cheaply, and preserves options: call a reasonable turn bet (pot odds are favorable), fold to big aggression, or take a free card if he checks back. Plan: check flop, call small/medium bets on later streets to try to hit the flush, and re‑evaluate if faced with heavy resistance.

Turn pairs the Qc with Qh, completing many heart flush draws on a already draw-heavy board (Kh Th Qh now). My 6s9h picks up a strong flush draw (9h gives me 9 outs to the flush) plus lingering gutshot straight draw potential (4 outs to J for 9TJQK). No pair or made hand, but excellent equity (~35-40% vs typical calling range). OOP vs button limper who checked flop, representing possible weakness, floats, or marginal made hands like mid pairs or uncoordinated broadway. Pot small (250), deep stacks. Donk betting semi-bluffs here for value from worse draws/pairs (e.g., smaller flush draws, T/x, Q/x, KQ), fold equity against air/floats, and to build pot with my strong equity when called. Sizing ~3/4 pot (175-200) pressures her wide limp range without overbetting OOP. Checking risks giving free card to her draws or allowing her to realize equity cheaply, while betting denies that and leverages fold equity on scary board. Consistent with aggressive image from prior hands (e.g., Hand 17 big pots won).

I have a made heart flush (2♥4♥) on Q♣ K♥ T♥ Q♥ — a very strong hand but not the absolute nuts (only beaten by A♥ + another heart). Elon’s small turn bet (200 into a ~250 pot) is consistent with a wide value/bluff range (Kx/Tx/Qx, slower-played strong hands, or a thin protection bet). Raising for value and to deny a free heart on the river is the +EV play: it extracts chips from single-pair and two-pair hands that will call and charges any one-heart combos that could make a flush on the river. I size up to a meaningful but non-committing amount (raise to 1200 total) — big enough to get value and fold equity, but leaves room to re-evaluate if he 3-bets/shoves (which would often represent the rare AhXh combos that beat me).

Facing a raise to 1200 after my turn donk bet of 200 into a small pot on a draw-heavy board that paired the Q and completed the heart flush draw. My 6s9h holds a strong flush draw (9 outs to the nuts K-high flush, since Kh on board) plus a gutshot straight draw to Broadway (4 outs to J). Approximately 13 outs or ~50% equity against her calling range of worse draws, floats, or marginal made hands, and still strong (~30-35%) even against value range including flushes (two hearts), two-pair (KQ, KT, QT), or sets. Pot odds are favorable: calling 1000 into 1650 pot (becomes 2650, ~27.5:1 or 27% needed, but we have more) with excellent implied odds given deep stacks (my 20k, her 9k) for river value if we hit. OOP but Minni's wide limp-check-flop range likely includes bluffs, weak pairs, and vulnerable made hands she can rep with this sizing. Raising (min 2200) risks bloating pot further vs strong hands while folding out worse; folding a premium draw after showing strength is -EV. Calling realizes equity, controls pot size, and sets up river check-fold/call/jam depending on board and her action. Consistent with aggressive image but disciplined play.

River Tc bricks completely, pairing the board's ten but missing my flush draw (9h with board's three hearts: Kh, Th, Qh) and gutshot to J. Hand 6s9h has zero showdown value—no pair, no straight, no flush—pure air on a paired Qs/Ts with K board (QQTTK). Minni's massive turn raise to 1200 over my small 200 semi-bluff bet into 250 pot suggests strong value: likely flush (Ahx, flush draw hit), two-pair (KQ, QT), sets (QQ, TT, KK unlikely from limp-check), or full house draw. Her button limp-check-flop range includes these, and huge sizing represents strength. Pot 2650, OOP first to act, effective her 9100. Betting bluff risks her calling/raising with better and bloating pot OOP; fold equity low post-turn call of her raise (looks like realized draw). Checking controls pot, induces bluffs/value bets we can fold profitably (prior call had equity/implied, now pure fold equity unnecessary). Consistent with disciplined play after realizing turn equity by calling; no free cards left but avoids spew. Long-term tournament: preserve stack vs aggressive opponents (Minni history: raises light but here committed).

Check. The river (Qc Kh Th Qh Tc) paired the board and now gives any Q or T in Elon’s hand a full house — those hands beat my 2♥4♥ flush. I have a strong but non‑nut K‑high heart flush (only A♥ and full houses beat me). Elon called my large turn raise, so his range plausibly includes Qx/Tx (now full houses), Kx, A♣/Ax and some heart combos; his river check is ambiguous (trap or medium strength). Betting here would only get thin value from Kx and some worse hands while risking being check‑raised or called by hands that already beat me. With deep stacks and a medium pot (~2.65k) I prefer to control the pot and take showdown — so I check.

I am on the button with K7o in a three-handed game. This is a standard opening hand from this position to put pressure on the blinds. Given the aggressive nature of both Elon and Minni in previous hands, I want to define the value of my hand early. A raise to 3x the big blind is a standard opening size that allows me to take control of the pot or win it pre-flop if the blinds fold.

Hand 2h6d is extremely weak offsuit trash with no straight or flush potential. Facing a button raise from Flash, who has been aggressive in prior hands. As SB, out of position postflop with deep stacks. Pot odds are about 2:1 (calling 250 into 700 effective if BB folds), but equity vs any reasonable button range is poor (~20-30%). Best to fold and preserve stack for better spots in a long tournament.

Flash opened to 300 from the button. Pot is 450 and I must call 200 more. My hand (5♦3♣) is very weak — offsuit, no meaningful blockers or high cards, and will be widely dominated by a BTN opening range. Calling requires ~44% equity (200 into a 450 pot) which this hand doesn’t have versus a standard open, and playing OOP will lead to difficult, low-expected-value spots. A 3‑bet bluff here is unnecessary and high-variance with little fold equity against a button opener. With a healthy 116 BB stack I’ll preserve chips and wait for a better spot. Therefore fold.

Preflop on the button in a 3-handed pot with Ac4c (suited Ace with good playability, nut flush potential, and Ace blocker). Blinds posted pot at 150, to call 100. In position, this is a standard raising hand to steal blinds or build pot in position. Opponents have shown willingness to call raises but also fold often (e.g., recent hands). Raising to 3x BB (300 total) is balanced sizing, applies pressure, and maintains initiative postflop. Limping is too passive; folding too tight for button range.

I have 7♣2♥ — essentially the worst starting hand. Elon opened to 300 from the button; I’m in the small blind and will be out of position against both the button and the big blind. Calling 250 more to play a multiway pot out of position with this hand is a clear negative-EV decision, and a 3-bet bluff here is unnecessary and high variance. Preserve my 11,600 stack for better spots. Fold.

In this hand, I am in the Big Blind with Queen-Five offsuit. Elon, on the Button, has raised to 300. With Minni folding the Small Blind, the action is on me. While I have decent pot odds to call (200 to win a total pot of 650), Q5o is a weak hand that is easily dominated by Elon's Button opening range, which likely includes any Ace, any King, better Queens, and most pairs. Playing out of position against an aggressive and well-stacked opponent like Elon with such a marginal hand is generally a losing strategy in the long run. I will fold and wait for a better spot.

5♣3♥ is a very weak, unplayable hand, especially multiway. Calling 100 into a 150 pot requires roughly 40% equity to break even; 5-3 offsuit has far less equity versus two unknown opponents, and I’d be in difficult postflop spots with little fold equity or real equity. A minimum raise to 200 is too small to credibly steal and will just invite calls or a 3-bet from Elon’s large stack. With deep stacks there will be better, more profitable spots to be aggressive — preserve chips and fold here.

I am in the Small Blind with King-Six suited (Kh 6h). After the Button (Minni) folds, the action is on me. In a blind-versus-blind scenario, King-high suited is a strong hand that should be played aggressively to capitalize on positional range advantages. Raising is preferred over calling to potentially win the pot immediately or take control of the hand. I will raise to 300, which is 3x the big blind, to apply pressure to Elon in the Big Blind.

Preflop in the big blind facing a raise to 300 from Flash in the small blind after Minni folded. My hand is 6c7h, offsuit one-gapper with poor playability. Pot is 400, calling 200 for pot odds of about 3:1 (25% equity needed). While BB defense can be wide, 67o has mediocre equity (~35-40% vs a loose SB opening range) and reverse position postflop. Flash has shown aggression but stacks are deep (my 19.5k effective vs his 8.45k). Folding preserves stack for better spots; no need to defend marginal trash here to maximize long-term EV over 150 hands.

I am on the button in a 3-handed game. Jack-Trey suited is a marginal hand, but in 3-handed play, positional advantage is crucial. Opening with a standard 3x raise (300) from the button allows me to put pressure on the blinds (Elon and Minni) and potentially take the pot pre-flop. If either opponent re-raises significantly, I can safely fold knowing I have plenty of chips remaining (over 80 big blinds).

Preflop from SB facing a button raise to 300 (3x BB). My hand 4h6s is offsuit, gapped, and weak with poor playability postflop. No position advantage, bad implied odds, and standard fold even with pot odds of ~1.8:1 effective (pot 450, call 250). Deep stacks don't justify speculative play here against a frequent raiser like Flash. Preserve stack for better spots.

Three-handed, facing a button open to 300. I hold A♣5♠ — a suited ace with good postflop playability (nut-flush and wheel possibilities) and reasonable equity vs a wide button opening range. Pot odds are attractive: I need roughly 30.8% equity to call (200/(450+200)), and with deep stacks I have good implied odds when I hit. Folding is too tight; a 3-bet would bloat the pot and often run into better aces or strong hands. I'll defend by calling to 300 and play the flop relatively straightforwardly (pot control, fold to heavy resistance when unimproved).

Flop Qd 9c Td with Ac5s — I have only Ace-high and no realistic draw (one club on board + Ac doesn't give a usable flush draw). The button's open range is wide and connects well with this texture (Qx, Tx, 9x, KJ, JT, connectors), so a c-bet OOP with a marginal hand will often run into hands that continue. With deep stacks I want to keep the pot controllable and evaluate turn action rather than bloating the pot out of position. Plan: check and fold to heavy aggression if I don't improve; call small turn bets if I pick up equity or if it’s clearly a bluff attempt; re-evaluate on any Ace/blank turn or if villain checks back.

I have flopped a monster draw with an open-ended straight draw (any King or Eight) and a flush draw (any diamond). With 15 outs to a likely winning hand, I have significant equity. By betting 400 into a 650 pot, I maintain my aggression as the pre-flop raiser and potentially win the pot immediately. If called, I have many ways to improve on the turn.

Call 400. I have A♣5♠ on Q♦9♣T♦ and am facing a small c‑bet of 400 into a 1050 pot. The call requires ~27.6% equity (400/(1050+400)). I have only Ace‑high and no real draw, and my raw two‑card chance to pair an A or 5 by the river (~24%) is slightly below that number — but that underestimates my real equity versus a wide BTN open/c‑bet range because I currently beat pure air (Kx, Jx, busted broadways) and I block some Ax combos. With deep stacks I want to keep the pot controlled and reevaluate on the turn rather than bloating the pot with a marginal hand. A raise would commit chips too often against better pairs or Ax; folding would be too tight given the pot odds. Plan: call the 400, fold to heavy turn/river aggression if I don’t improve, extract value or turn the hand into a bluff catcher if I pair an Ace, and consider a delayed c‑bet on safe blank turns if villain checks.

Check. The turn (Tc) paired the board and heavily improves many hands that beat my Ace‑high (Tx combos, Qx that now fear a draw, sets/full houses). With Ac5s I have only 6 clean outs (3 Aces + 3 Fives) — roughly a 13% chance to pair on the river — and no realistic flush or straight draw. Flash’s line (open, flop c‑bet, then checking the turn) can be either weakness or pot control with a lot of hands that still beat me, so betting here would bloat the pot with a marginal holding and will often get called or raised by better hands. Pot control is correct: check to see the river and fold to meaningful aggression; only call small river bets or continue if I actually pair an Ace/5 or the river card makes a plausible bluff-catching spot.

The turn card is the Ten of Clubs, which pairs the middle card of the flop. While I have a massive draw (open-ended straight draw plus a flush draw, totaling 14 outs), the board pairing makes the situation more dangerous, as Minni could have a Ten for trips or a Queen for two pair. Since Minni checked to me, checking behind is the most sensible play. This allows me to see the river for free and potentially realize my equity without risking more chips against a hand that might not fold. If I hit one of my many outs on the river, I can then value bet.

Board Qd 9c Td Tc Kh, I hold A♣5♠ — I missed: no pair and no backdoor draws. Flash’s line (button open, flop c‑bet 400, then checking the turn) is consistent with a lot of made hands (Qx, Tx, 9x, Kx) or a pot‑control line with a medium pair; he checked the river as well, so I have very little fold equity and almost no value hands I can get paid by. Pot is 1450 and my hand only beats pure bluffs. Betting is thin EV and a raise is clearly a mistake. I will check and take the showdown; fold to any meaningful river bet (only consider calling a tiny pure-bluff-sized bet).

On the river, the King of Hearts completes my King-high straight (9-T-J-Q-K). While this is a strong hand, the board is highly coordinated with two Tens and several high cards. My opponent, Minni, has checked twice (turn and river) after calling my flop bet. This could indicate a marginal hand like a Queen or a Nine, but it could also be a trap with a Ten (trips) or a Jack (chopping). Furthermore, any Ace-Jack combination would make a higher, Ace-high straight. Given that a bet is unlikely to be called by a worse hand (like a Queen) and might only be called by hands that chop or beat me (trips/boats/higher straights), checking behind to realize my showdown value is the most prudent professional play.

3-handed No-Limit Hold'em on the button first to act pre-flop. K7o (offsuit) is within the standard opening range for this position and dynamic, as button ranges are wide first-in. King blocker, decent high card value, good playability post-flop. Raising takes initiative, builds pot, and forces blinds to play OOP. Standard raise size of 3x BB (300) is appropriate given deep stacks and history of similar opens. Folding too tight; limping/calling passive and puts us OOP.

I’m in the small blind with 9♦6♦ facing a button open to 300. I’ve already posted 50, so calling requires +250 (300 total commitment). With ~108bb effective and a BTN opening range likely fairly wide, 9♦6♦ is a playable, speculative suited hand with good implied odds and multi-way potential. 3-betting this hand is too weak (no fold equity/value to justify inflating an OOP pot); folding is too nitty. I’ll call to see a flop, keep pot control, and re-evaluate based on flop texture and the big blind’s action.

Q6o is a weak hand to defend in a 3-way pot. Elon raised from the button and Minni called from the small blind. While the pot odds are roughly 3.5:1, the hand has poor playability out of position and is frequently dominated by the ranges of the other two players. Folding is the prudent play here to preserve chips for better spots.

I have 9♦6♦ on J♣ 9♣ J♥ — that’s two pair (Jacks and Nines) with a weak 6-kicker. Heads-up to a BTN open, Elon’s range is wide so I’m often ahead of single-pair hands and many Kx/Qx combos, but I’m behind any J (trips/quads), 99, JJ or QQ+/pocket-pair combinations that make better two-pair. Out of position, checking hands like this surrenders initiative and gives free cards to club draws and overcards; betting extracts value from worse one-pair and 9x hands and protects my hand. I’ll make a standard protection/value bet about half the pot to charge draws and get called by worse. If he raises big, that usually polarizes to a jack or a premium pocket pair and I’ll re-evaluate (often folding to a large check-raise, calling smaller raises depending on sizing).

Flop board Jc9cJh is paired top pair with flush draw potential. Minni leads 350 into 1050 pot from SB after calling our button open pre-flop. Our K7o (7c Kd) flops a flush draw (9 outs with 7c, exactly two clubs on board) plus king overcard. No pair or straight draw. Position is key with deep stacks (19k effective). Pot odds excellent: calling 350 to 1400 pot (~4:1), equity ~25-35% vs her leading range (Jx, 99, draws, Ax). Folding too tight given live draw and initiative; raising risks bloating pot OOP for her or getting snapped by sets/Jx. Calling realizes equity cheaply, maintains control, allows turn play: fold weak draws, bet/fold/raise based on board/runout. Consistent with aggressive pre-flop image.

I have two pair (Jacks and Nines) on J♣ 9♣ J♥ 4♣ — a very strong but vulnerable hand (beats most single-pair hands and some draws, loses to trips J or a full house with pocket 9s). Pot is 1400 after Elon called my flop bet. The turn 4♣ adds a third club (flush is possible only if villain holds both clubs), so the main threats remain Jx/99. I want to extract value from worse hands (Kx, Qx, single jacks that played passively, 9x, and draws) and deny free rivers, while keeping the pot manageable in case of a check-raise. I’ll bet ~50% pot (700) to charge calls from worse and fold to a large, polarized raise (which I’ll interpret as trips/full house).

Turn card 4c completes our club flush draw from the flop, giving us a jack-high flush (Jc, 9c, 7c, 4c). The board is extremely wet: paired jacks with a completed flush and straight draw possibilities (e.g., T8, Q10). Minni has donk-bet flop (350 into ~1050) and now turn (700 into 1400, pot now 2100). Her range: calling SB vs BTN open includes Jx (AJ, KJ, QJ, JT), sets, two-pair combos, flush draws that hit or missed, bluffs. We block KJ somewhat with Kd. Our flush is strong but vulnerable to higher flushes (Tc+, Qc, Kc, Ac). However, pot odds to call 700 into 2100 (~25% needed) are fine, but raising extracts value from worse hands (non-nut flushes, trips, overpairs), applies pressure with fold equity on draws/missed bluffs, and denies equity to any remaining straight draws. Position allows control. Deep stacks (her 9550 behind), but tournament phase favors aggression. Raise to 2100 (3x her bet, pot-sized after call) balances value/protection without overcommitting. Folding/calling too passive given strength.

I have two pair (Jacks and Nines: 9♦6♦ on Jc 9c Jh 4c). Elon’s turn raise to 2,100 after my 700 is large and polarizing — his range includes very strong value hands (trip Jacks, pocket 9s or 4s turning into full houses, even quads) and now-made club flushes (two clubs in hand), plus a smaller number of bluffs/semi-bluffs. Folding would concede value vs worse Kx/Qx/9x hands; raising is unattractive because his sizing is polar and I don’t block his strongest combos — I’d be bloating the pot OOP with a vulnerable holding. Pot is 4,200 and calling 1,400 (total 2,100 committed) gives ~25% immediate pot odds. Against his full turn-raise range my raw equity is marginal vs only the made-value combos, but his check-call on the flop means his range still contains draws and weaker pairs I beat. I’ll call to control the pot and see the river, then re-evaluate: if I hit a full house (J or 9) I’ll extract; if the river completes a club or is a blank and Elon fires a large bet, I’ll fold to polarized aggression.

Check. The river (Jc 9c Jh 4c 5c) leaves me with two pair (Jacks & Nines) but it also completes a very scary club-heavy board: with four clubs on board any single club in Elon’s hand now makes a flush. His line (button open preflop, check‑call on the flop to my protection bet, then a very large turn check‑raise to 2,100) is highly polarized toward strong value (trip Jacks, a set of 9s, 44 trips/full house, or hands that can reasonably turn into or already had strong holdings). Many of those hands beat me; some single‑club combos that didn’t have two clubs on the turn now make a flush on the river and also beat me.

Betting for thin value is unlikely to work here — most worse hands that would call a river bet (Kx/Qx/weak 9x) are unlikely to have continued in the face of his huge turn raise. Betting just bloats a pot I’m likely behind in; checking lets him show aggression and gives me the option to fold to polarized large river bets while still calling small river spots that could be bluffs. Plan: check and fold to a large value-sized bet; consider calling a small-to-medium bet if it’s a thin value or obvious blocker bluff.

River 5c brings the fourth club, pairing low cards but keeping our 7c flush intact (Jc-9c-7c-5c-4c). Board extremely draw-heavy with paired jacks and flush; non-flush hands (Jx trips, overpairs) crushed. Villain's line: called pre vs BTN open (SB wide), donk flop paired board (polarized: Jx/sets/draws), donk turn into flush-completing card, called our 3x raise (2100 total into ~2100 pot). Her check river after large call suggests caution with medium Jx, lower flushes, or missed draws/bluffs unwilling to bomb OOP. We block Kd (possible Kc flush), strong vs her non-nut range. Pot 5600, effective stacks ~8k; half-pot value bet (2800) extracts max from worse (lower flushes like 6c-3c, stubborn trips like AJs/KQs without 6+ club, Ax clubs <7? unlikely but possible), fold equity vs air/missed. Checking passive, forfeits value in tournament ICM-neutral spot. Consistent w/ aggressive image built this hand/history. 2800 sizes well (potentially ~36% pot odds for her call).

Board is Jc 9c Jh 4c 5c; I have two pair (Jacks and Nines) but the board is very club‑heavy and Elon's line (check‑call flop, very large turn raise, small river bet) is polarized toward strong value (trips/sets/full houses) with some bluffs/semi‑bluffs mixed in. His river sizing is small (2,800 into a 8,400 pot ≈33%), so calling only requires ~25% equity to be break‑even. Folding surrenders too much value vs Kx/Qx/weak 9x that will still call a small river bet; raising OOP is dangerous — I don’t block his strongest combos and would be inflating a pot where I’m often behind. Given the pot odds and the likelihood his river contains a mix of thin value and bluffs, calling to realize equity and see showdown is the highest EV play. I will call 2,800 (leaving ~5,350 behind).

K9o on the button in a 3-handed game is a good hand to open — decent playability and excellent positional advantage. Calling 100 is too passive and gives up fold equity; a standard 3x BB open to 300 pressures the blinds, keeps the pot manageable with our ~53bb stack, and allows us to play postflop in position. Plan: if one or both call, proceed cautiously on the flop and use position; fold to a large, committed 3-bet, consider calling small 3-bets to realize equity.

I am in the Small Blind with Qs 3c, which is a weak hand even for three-handed play. The Button (Minni) has opened for 300. Calling out of position with a marginal hand like Q3 offsuit is generally a losing play, and raising would be a low-percentage bluff against a relatively short stack who might feel committed. Given the strength of my hand and my position, folding is the most prudent decision to preserve my stack.

Preflop in BB with 6c Th (offsuit, mediocre hand) facing a button raise to 300 after SB fold. Pot is 450, calling 200 for ~31% pot odds. Button range is wide (steal-heavy based on Minni's history of frequent raises: hands 5,9,10,13,19,25), but T6o has poor playability postflop, low equity (~30-35% vs wide range), and dominated often. No strong reason to defend speculative hand deepstacked; preserve stack for better spots. Standard fold.

I am on the button with Qh2c. In a three-handed game, the button should open a wide range of hands, but Q2o is exceptionally weak and lacks playability if called. Both Elon and Minni have shown a willingness to defend their blinds and play aggressively post-flop. Given that Q2o is at the bottom of the range and the risk-to-reward ratio for a steal attempt is unfavorable against these specific opponents, folding is the most disciplined and standard professional play here.

T3o is a very weak offsuit hand with poor connectivity, low high card, and no flush potential. From SB vs BB after BTN fold, there is some dead money (150 in pot), and calling 50 more gives pot odds of 3:1 (150 to win + my 50), but postflop playability is terrible against BB's wider defending range. Minni has shown loose calling tendencies in prior hands but position and initiative postflop matter. Raising as a steal risks being called by better hands, and limping invites multiway pots or difficult spots OOP. With deep stacks (25k effective vs Minni's 5.4k), folding preserves stack for better spots in this long tournament (27/150). Standard solver play folds T3o in this spot to avoid -EV engagements.

AJ offsuit is a premium opening hand from the button in a 3-handed game. With deep stacks (mine 25k, opponents 5.5k and 9.35k), raising first-in is standard to build the pot, apply pressure on blinds, and take initiative. Standard raise size is 3x the big blind to 300, which is profitable given the hand's equity and fold equity. Folding or limping is too weak; this hand plays well post-flop.

I have pocket Jacks (JJ) in the small blind facing a button open to 300. JJ is a strong hand here and, with ~55BB effective, I want to 3-bet for value and to isolate the button rather than just call and play out of position. A 3-bet to 1200 (≈4x the open) is large enough to force many weaker hands and speculative calls (and to discourage a multiway pot with the big blind) while leaving plenty of stack behind to play postflop. Calling would concede initiative and invite difficult OOP decisions; folding is too weak. If called I'll proceed to c-bet appropriate flops and re-evaluate if I face heavy resistance or a 4-bet. Therefore I raise to 1200 to maximize value and fold equity.

I am in the Big Blind with 8d 2c, which is a very weak hand. Given that there has been an open raise from the Button and a significant 3-bet from the Small Blind, there is no strategic reason to commit any chips to this pot. I will fold and wait for a better opportunity.

AJ offsuit remains strong facing a SB 3-bet in a 3-handed spot, especially with position. Minni's 4x 3-bet sizing after my button open is aggressive but her SB range includes many Ax, broadways, and pairs that AJ blocks and has good equity against (roughly 40-45% vs typical 3-bet range). Pot odds are favorable: calling 900 into a 2500 pot (post-call) gives ~36% implied odds needed, but position and her 43BB stack (effective ~31BB after her bet) allow post-flop control and fold equity on later streets. Raising risks a shove from her shorter stack with worse hands, while folding is too tight given history (she's shown aggression like in Hand 19). Calling keeps the pot manageable, realizes equity in position, and maintains deep stack leverage over 122 remaining hands.

Flop Q♠ 9♠ T♠ is very wet and highly unfavorable for my hand. I hold J♣ J♦ — a single pair with no spade — and many of Elon's reasonable cold-call hands (two spades, KJ/J8 straights, Qx/Tx/9x, sets) either already beat me or have strong equity. I do, however, block a lot of the straight combos by holding both J's, so my equity isn't zero. Because I'm out of position and the board texture massively favors the caller, I don't want to bloat the pot with a marginal made hand. Checking controls the pot, keeps weaker hands and bluffs in his range, and allows me to call reasonable c-bets to realize equity while folding to heavy aggression. Plan: check — call small-to-medium continuation bets (~≤1/2 pot) and re-evaluate on the turn; fold to large polarizing barrels or check-raises unless the turn improves me to two pair/trips or is a very safe card.

Flop Qs9sTs gives me the nuts straight with 9-T-J-Q using my Js, plus a nut flush redraw (four spades: 9s-Ts-Qs-Js) and gutshot to higher straight/broadway. This is a monster hand in position after calling Minni's SB 3-bet pre. Her check after aggression suggests possible missed draw, weak pair, or air, but her range includes sets (QQ,99,TT unlikely), two-pair (QT,Q9,T9), combo draws (KJss,KQs, etc.), or bluffs. Pot is 2500, her stack 4350 (effective), mine deep at 23.8k. Betting for value and protection against her draws (flush/straight draws on this board). Sizing to 1700 (~2/3 pot) balances value from worse made hands/draws while denying equity and setting up turns where I can barrel or control. Checking risks giving free cards to draws or check-raise, suboptimal with this strength. Raise/fold equity unnecessary; thin value and realization key over 122 hands remaining.

Flop Q♠ 9♠ T♠ vs my J♣ J♦ — a very spade-heavy, dangerous board. Elon cold‑called my 3‑bet preflop so his range is fairly strong and contains a lot of spades, Q/T/9 combos and some sets. His bet of 1,700 into a 4,200 pot is relatively small (~40% pot) — cheap enough to call to see a turn. I’m out of position and only have one pair with no spade, so I don’t want to bloat the pot with a marginal hand by check‑raising or over‑raising; that would get me committed against many hands that already beat me. I also block J combos which removes a lot of straight combos (KJ, J8), so my pair still has some showdown value and I can realise equity cheaply. Plan: call the 1,700 to control the pot and re‑evaluate on the turn — fold to large turn barrels or check‑raises unless I improve to two‑pair/trips or the turn is a safe card that changes the ranges drastically.

Elon checked to me on the K♥ turn. I have J♣J♦ — a marginal one-pair hand that is behind most Kx/Qx/sets and vulnerable to spade flushes and the straights that were possible on the flop. He cold‑called my 3‑bet and barreled a relatively small flop, so his range contains a lot of hands that already beat me or have good equity. I also block many J‑containing straight combos by holding both jacks, so my pair has some showdown value. With the pot at 5,900 and only 2,650 behind, betting here would commit a large portion of my stack OOP against a range that is often ahead; checking controls the pot and lets me realize equity cheaply. Plan: check and evaluate the river — call small/medium river bets for showdown when appropriate, fold to large polarizing bets, or value/raise only if I improve to two‑pair/trips.

Turn Kh completes Broadway straights (KQJTx) but my AhJs still holds the nut straight (9s-Ts-Js-Qs-Kh for 9-T-J-Q-K, no higher possible) plus nut flush redraw (four spades out, 9 outs immediate). Board heavy with spades, so her flop call range (after SB 3bet pre) includes flush draws (KJss, AsXs), straight draws (KJ, AQ), pairs (QQ, TT, 99, Qx), two-pair/combos, bluffs. Kh bricks most spade draws (hearts), pairs board no flush, but gives her possible Broadway if she had KJ/AQ. Her check after flop check-call suggests weakness: missed draw, marginal made hand unwilling to lead, or trap (unlikely given history). Pot 5900, her stack 2650 (shorter, ~27BB effective pre but now committed), mine deep 22k. Betting all-in 2650 (~45% pot) extracts max value from her worse made hands (smaller straights like QT/JT, two-pair Q9/QT/T9, top pair), draws chasing (now poor odds post-Kh), and avoids check-raise/fold equity issues. Checking risks free river card to redraws or her improving; pot control unnecessary with nuts. Over 122 hands left, growing stack by eliminating her short stack maximizes EV in tournament.

I hold Jc Jd on Qs 9s Ts Kh. Elon cold‑called my 3‑bet preflop, bet the flop and now barrels the turn for 2,650 into a pot of 8,550 — I must call my last 2,650 to continue. That call requires ~23.7% equity to break even (2,650 / (8,550+2,650)). Given his line (cold‑call 3‑bet, flop bet, turn barrel) his range is heavily weighted to hands that already beat me: KQ/KT/K9 two‑pair, Qx/Tx/9x top pairs, sets (TT/99/QQ) and possible two‑spade flushes. Versus those value hands I have very few outs (essentially the two remaining jacks ≈ 4.3% vs most two‑pair/sets and effectively 0% vs a made spade flush). I do block some J‑containing straight combos (KJ/J8), which helps a little, but my weighted equity against his plausible continuing range is well below the ~23.7% needed. Calling would commit my entire stack out of position in a spot my earlier plan explicitly said to fold to a large, polarizing barrel unless I improved. Fold and preserve chips for a better spot.

Button with A5s and 26.5bb — good playability and a blocker to the nut ace. In 3-handed play I should be opening a wide range; raising gains fold equity and lets me play postflop in position. Min-raising to 200 is available but a standard 3xBB to 300 is cleaner vs these table tendencies (Elon raises a lot preflop; 300 reduces limp/callers and looks like a normal open). Plan: if faced with a large 3-bet (e.g. ~900+ total) I’ll fold; against a smaller squeeze (~400–600) I may call and use position to play for flush/ace-high lines. If called, c-bet favorable boards and press advantage with position and hearts draw. Raise to 300 to take the initiative.

I am in the Small Blind with 4c 5c. Minni (Button) has raised to 3x the big blind (300). She is playing a relatively short stack of about 23.5 BBs. While 45s is a decent suited connector, playing it out of position against a short stack reduces my implied odds significantly. Additionally, Elon is in the Big Blind with a massive stack and has shown extreme aggression in previous hands (like Hand 17 and Hand 25). If I call, I risk Elon squeezing me out of the pot before I can see a flop, or being forced to play a multi-way pot out of position with a speculative hand. Given the tournament context and the players involved, folding is the most disciplined play to preserve my stack.

Preflop in the big blind facing a min-raise to 300 from Minni on the button, who has a short stack of 2350 (23.5BB). Q8o is a decent speculative hand with a queen blocker and potential for top pair or draws. Pot odds are excellent: calling an additional 200 into a pot of 450 provides about 2.25:1 (31% equity required). Against a wide button opening range from a short stack, Q8o realizes roughly 40-45% equity. Despite being out of position, my deep stack (279 BB effective) allows flexible postflop play, controlling pot size or folding to aggression if missed. Defending wide here maintains range balance and exploits Minni's aggression seen in prior hands.

Flop board is 7c Th Jd, giving me queen-high with no pair, no straight draw (gutshot would require 9 for open-ender, but 8 doesn't connect directly), and only backdoor flush draws (hearts and clubs). Out of position as first to act into a pot of 650 against Minni's short-stacked button range, which hits this board decently often (JT, T7, J7, pairs, draws like 89, Q9, etc.). My preflop defending range includes many floats and speculative hands, so checking maintains balance. No strong reason to lead as bluff here—board texture favors her range slightly, and deep vs her 23BB, better to control pot OOP and fold to her continuation bet, realizing fold equity without risking chips on a bluff lead or check-raise against her polarized short-stack aggression seen in prior hands (e.g., Hand 19, 28). Check to pot control and induce bluffs/checks.

I opened preflop to 300 from the button with A♥5♥ so my range is wide and I have position. Flop J♦ T♥ 7♣ gives me just Ace-high but I have a backdoor heart draw (T♥ on board) and the Ace blocker, while many of Elon's calling combos (KQ, K9, Q9, many unpaired hands) miss this board. Elon checked the flop — showing either weakness or a draw — so I should c-bet to seize the initiative and fold out a lot of his missed hands. I don't want to bloat the pot with only Ace-high, so I choose a controlled half-pot-sized c-bet to generate fold equity but preserve stack depth for later streets. Bet 300 into 650: if he folds I take the pot; if he calls I will reevaluate on the turn and barrel on favorable cards. If he check-raises large I will fold this marginal hand rather than commit.

Facing a 300 bet into 950 pot (~1/3 pot) on a coordinated Jd Th 7c flop as OOP with Qh8c (queen-high, no pair, no straight draw, only weak backdoor heart/club potential). Minni's button min-raise and c-bet range on this draw-heavy board includes frequent Jx/Tx/97s/89s/Q9s for value/draws plus bluffs, heavily favoring her over my trash holding (~20-30% equity vs polarized range). Preflop call was for balance/exploiting wide opens, but postflop check was for pot control expecting to fold to c-bet, preserving deep stack (277BB eff) vs her short 20BB. Calling OOP bloats pot with no equity realization or fold equity, risks turn shove; raising as bluff unbalances range and exploits little given her committed sizing/commitment. Standard fold realizes EV by avoiding spew, focuses on +EV spots later.

I am on the button with 5-2 offsuit, which is one of the weakest starting hands in poker. While I have positional advantage, Minni is short-stacked with only 29 big blinds and has shown aggression in previous hands. Raising with such a weak hand risks a shove from the short stack or a re-raise from the chip leader, Elon. There is no reason to commit chips with this hand in a three-handed game when I have a healthy stack of 93 big blinds.

Preflop in the small blind, heads-up against Minni in the big blind after Flash folds on the button. My hand is 5s2c, one of the worst possible starting hands with no connectivity, low cards, and offsuit. The pot is 150, and I need to call 50 more to complete. While completing is sometimes considered in SB vs BB with marginal hands for pot odds, 52o has zero playability postflop - no straight potential really, dominated often, and poor implied odds even against Minni's short stack of 2900. Raising as a bluff is possible due to Minni's shorter stack and her history of aggression, but fold equity is uncertain given her short stack might defend wide, and this hand has no equity if called. Tournament is early (hand 30/150), my stack is deep at 27k, no need to gamble with trash. Preserving stack for better spots is optimal. Standard play is to fold.

78s is a strong suited connector on the button in a 3-handed pot that appears limped (SB Minni likely limped 50, BB Flash checked, pot 150, to call 100). Standard play is to raise wide from the button to take initiative, isolate weaker hands, and build the pot with a playable hand that has good postflop potential. Raising to 3BB (300 total) is a good size given pot odds and stacks: Minni has ~30BB and may fold or shove, Flash ~92BB can defend wide but 78s plays well multiway or heads-up. Folding is too tight 3-handed; flat-calling denies dead money and risks multiway pot without initiative.

I’m in the SB with A♥Q♥ (30bb effective) facing a button open to 300. This is a premium hand — I want to avoid a multiway pot with the BB and take the initiative. A sized 3-bet to 1,200 (leaving ~1,800 behind) pressures the opener, folds out many weaker holdings, and still leaves a comfortable stack for postflop play if called. Folding is too tight and calling is too passive (it lets the BB in cheaply), while an all-in is overly polarizing. I’ll 3-bet to isolate and play the hand to win the pot heads-up or with initiative postflop.

I am in the Big Blind with Ten-Four offsuit, which is a very weak hand. Elon opened from the Button, and Minni 3-bet from the Small Blind to 1200, leaving herself with only 650 chips behind. This 3-bet range from a short stack is typically very strong, and I have no reason to commit chips to a pot where I am likely dominated and facing a near-all-in situation. Folding is the best play to preserve my stack.

78o is a marginal hand facing a 4x 3-bet from short-stacked SB Minni who has only 650 chips left after her 1200. Her 3-bet range is strong (QQ+, AKo, AQs+), and 78o has poor equity (~25-35%) against it heads-up. Calling 900 into 1600 (needing ~36% equity) risks her jamming the 650 on flop with position out of position OOP, turning it into a bloated pot with a speculative hand. Raising/shoving has reverse implied odds vs her range where she continues only with better. Folding preserves stack for better spots; 3-handed button opens wide, but defends selectively vs 3-bets. History shows Minni aggressive but this sizing commits her, likely polarized strong.

3♣2♣ (3h2c) is a very weak hand, but calling 100 (1BB) from the button is cheap relative to my 3450 stack (~34.5BB) and preserves position postflop. I’ll see a flop cheaply to leverage positional advantage and attempt to steal or realize equity; I’ll fold to significant preflop aggression (3-bet/large raise) since this hand plays poorly out of position and has low equity. Calling keeps the pot small and gives me options on the flop.

I am holding AdTd in the Small Blind, which is a very strong premium hand in a 3-handed game. Minni has limped from the button, and Elon is in the Big Blind. To extract value from my hand and isolate against the limper, I will put in a standard raise to 400. This puts pressure on Minni's wide limping range and prevents Elon from seeing a free flop with a potentially weak hand.

ATo is a premium hand from the BB facing a SB isolation raise after a BTN limp. The hand has excellent equity, blockers to strong ace ranges (AA, AK), and plays well multiway or heads-up. Limper is likely weak, SB raise range is wide. 3-betting isolates the limper, applies pressure on raiser, and builds the pot with position. Sizing to 1400 (3.5x the raise) is standard, denies equity to weaker hands, and sets up postflop aggression. Folding or calling is too passive given our deep stack and strong holding.

3♥2♣ on the button is a very weak, highly dominated hand. I called 100 earlier to see a flop cheaply, but Flash’s raise to 400 and Elon’s 3-bet to 1,400 changes the decision. To continue I must call 1,300 into a 1,900 pot (total pot after my call ≈ 3,200), so I need roughly 40.6% equity to break even — far more than 3‑2 off can realistically expect versus a 3‑bet range. Multiway action (Flash may still be in) only reduces my equity further. Calling would commit ~39% of my stack and leave me short‑stacked (~20.5BB) with a junk hand and high likelihood of facing further aggression. Raising or shoving here is equally unjustified. I’ll fold and preserve chips for a better spot.

I am holding AdTd, which is a premium hand in 3-handed play. After I raised to 400, Elon (the chip leader) 3-bet to 1400. While his range could include strong pairs or better Aces, he has been very aggressive throughout the tournament. Folding here would be too tight given my hand's equity and the pot odds. Calling allows me to see a flop and realize my hand's potential while keeping my options open, rather than committing my entire stack with a 4-bet/5-bet shove pre-flop. With an SPR of approximately 2.5 on the flop, I will be looking to play a high-variance pot if I connect with the board.

I have missed the flop with my Ace-Ten suited, and the board of Jack-high doesn't particularly favor my range after calling a 3-bet out of position. Elon is an aggressive player and likely has a range consisting of strong overcards or pocket pairs. Checking is the standard play to see what he does next and manage the pot size, given my Ace-high has some showdown value but is vulnerable to further pressure.

Preflop 3-bet with ATo (As blocker) got called by SB after isolating limper. Flop 2h8hJs is draw-heavy (hearts flush draw, open-enders like 9T/T9). As OOP aggressor, c-bet range includes strong pairs (AJ+, 88/22 rare), sets, and some bluffs. My ace-high blocks top of ranges (AA/AK/AQ), nut flush draw. Flash's check suggests weakness (no top pair likely, or slowplay), so bet denies equity to draws/floats, extracts value from weaker Ax/Jx. Deep stacks (his 7800), pot 2900; 2000 (~2/3 pot) offers fold equity vs draws while pot-controlling if called. Checking OOP risks giving free cards to improve/draw out.

I am currently holding Ace-Ten of diamonds. Pre-flop, I raised to 400 and called Elon's 3-bet to 1400. The flop comes Jack-high with two hearts, and I have completely missed. Elon has now fired a 2000-chip bet into a 2900-chip pot (nearly 70% pot). Given Elon's high aggression throughout the tournament and the fact that he 3-bet me pre-flop, his range likely includes strong hands like overpairs (QQ-AA), top pair (AJ, KJ), or big draws. My Ace-high has very little equity here, and I am out of position against the chip leader. Peeling a card with just overcards and no significant draw on this texture is too expensive and risky. I will fold to preserve my stack for a more favorable situation.

I am on the button with Qs Jh, a solid three-handed starting hand. The action is currently 100 to call as the small and big blinds are posted. Minni, in the big blind, is quite short-stacked (3250), while Elon is the chip leader. Given my position and the strength of my hand relative to a 3-way range, I will raise to 300 to take control of the pot and put pressure on the blinds.

Preflop from the small blind facing a button raise to 300 (3BB). My hand is 6c7c, suited connectors with good playability and implied odds given deep effective stacks (Flash 75BB, Minni 32BB). Button range is wide, especially from Flash who raises frequently based on history. Calling 250 more (total 300 committed) into a pot that will be 700 heads-up offers excellent implied odds (~2.8:1 direct, but postflop potential much better). Equity realization OOP is decent multi-way or heads-up vs. polarized button range (~40% equity vs. typical 35-40% button open). Folding too tight; 3-betting risks playing OOP with marginal hand postflop. Prefer calling to speculate profitably.

Facing a button raise to 300 with the small blind already calling, I’m in the big blind with K♣2♠ — a very weak, poorly playable hand that will often be dominated. The pot is 700 and I must call 200, which requires ~22% equity to break even (200 / (700+200)). In a three-way pot against a raiser and a caller my actual equity with K2o is well below that and I’ll be out of position postflop. A min‑raise to 500 as a bluff is unattractive: I have poor blockers, the SB is already committed and could call or 3‑bet, and I’d be playing postflop out of position with a weak hand. With ~32bb effective I should preserve chips for better spots. Fold.

Flop of 8s Ks 4c completely misses my 6c7c suited connectors. I have a weak gutshot straight draw to the 5 (4 outs) and a backdoor club flush draw (needing two running clubs). No pair, no strong equity against Flash's button raising range, which includes many Kx, suited broadways, and flush/straight draws given the spade-heavy board. OOP first to act in a 700 pot with only ~15% equity (gutshot) and minimal fold equity if bluffing, betting is -EV as Flash calls wide with top pair/draws and can raise. Checking realizes equity by getting a free turn card if he checks (possible with missed draws), or allows controlled fold to a bet. Tournament stage favors discipline over speculative aggression with deep stacks; preserve stack for better spots. Plan: check-fold to bet (unless tiny) or check-call small if pot odds allow chasing gutshot.

As the pre-flop aggressor on the button, I have a range advantage on this King-high board. A King-high texture typically favors the raiser over the caller in the small blind. I will place a continuation bet of 300 (approximately 43% of the pot) to exert pressure on Elon. This bet aims to win the pot immediately against hands that missed the board, such as small pairs or suited connectors, while also keeping the initiative if the hand proceeds to the turn.

Flop of 8s Ks 4c completely whiffs my 6c7c suited connectors. Only a weak gutshot to the 5 (4 outs, ~9% equity to river) and negligible backdoor club draw. Facing a 300 c-bet into 700 pot from Flash's wide button range, which connects well with Kx (top pair frequent), spade draws (Ks8s especially strong), sets, and two-pair combos. Pot odds are ~3.3:1 (300 into 1000), but I need ~23% equity for a call; I have far less (~9-10%) and poor playability OOP if I hit (often dominated). Raising has no fold equity vs. sticky button who cbets light but calls/raises with value/draws. Check-folding is disciplined, preserves deep stack (285BB) for premium spots in tournament. Consistent with pre-flop plan to speculate profitably but fold to misses.

3-handed on the button, A7o is a profitable opening hand with excellent position, ace high, and good playability postflop. Standard raise size of 3x the BB to 300 takes down the blinds often or builds a pot when called. We have deep stacks relative to pot, and fold equity against Minni's short stack (32BB) and Flash. History shows opponents fold to button aggression frequently. Limping or calling is suboptimal without position advantage exploitation.

I have Q♠T♣ in the small blind facing a button open to 300. This is a playable, well-blocking hand vs a wide button opening in a 3‑handed game — good equity vs a wide range and good flop playability, but it's marginal for a pure 3‑bet value. Calling 250 keeps the pot manageable and preserves my stack (32 BB) so I can maneuver postflop out of position; a min‑raise to 500 is a small, noncommittal 3‑bet that often invites a larger 4‑bet from the very deep button (Elon), which I don't want to face with QTs. Plan: call to see a flop, proceed aggressively if I hit (top pair, strong draws) or play cautiously / fold to heavy pressure on dry boards.

I am holding Jc 6c in the big blind. Elon raised from the button and Minni called from the small blind. I am getting 3.5:1 pot odds (200 to call into a 700 pot) and I am closing the action pre-flop. My hand has decent playability in a multi-way pot due to being suited. Given the attractive odds and the potential to flop a strong draw or two pair, calling is the most standard and profitable play here.

Flop: A♣ 8♣ 3♣; I hold Q♠ T♣. I have no pair but a very strong club draw — there are 9 clubs left, so ~19% to hit on the turn and ~35% to make a flush by the river — plus some extra equity from pairing the Q or T. Checked-to in position (OOP vs both opponents), this is a textbook semi-bluff: I want to build the pot when I hit and try to take it down now against hands that missed the Ace or don’t have clubs. A medium-sized c-bet (about half pot) balances fold equity and pot control — it pressures hands like Kx, medium pairs, and single-club/air that would otherwise see free cards, while not committing me with only a draw. Plan if action changes: fold to a large raise (I’m OOP with only a draw), call small isolation bets to see the turn, and if called I will continue aggressively on a completed club (value) or give up to heavy resistance on blank turns. Bet 450 into a 900 pot as a semi-bluff.

I have flopped a Jack-high flush on a monochrome board (Ac 8c 3c). This is a very strong made hand, but it is vulnerable to any higher club (Kc or Qc) appearing on the turn or river. Minni has led out for 450 into a 900 pot, which likely indicates top pair (Ax), a set, or a high club draw. Given Minni's relatively short stack (2500), I want to raise now to extract value from her weaker made hands and charge any high club draws. Raising also forces Elon to decide if his button-raising range is strong enough to continue against two opponents on a coordinated board. I will raise to 1100 to build the pot and protect my equity.

Preflop raiser on the button with Ad7c on Ac8c3c flop: top pair (aces) with a weak 7 kicker PLUS a strong flush draw (7c gives us 9 clean outs to the flush). This is a monster hand with excellent playability, ~35%+ equity against typical bet/raise ranges (sets, two-pair, flushes, strong draws). Board is highly coordinated (monotone clubs, possible straights), Minni's small bet suggests a draw or weak Ax, Flash's raise reps strength but could be overplaying draws or floats. We have deep position, huge fold equity especially vs short-stacked Minni (2500 behind), and can isolate Flash if he continues. Calling is passive and allows free cards/multiway; folding is spew. Raising ~3x Flash's 1100 builds a pot for value, applies max pressure pre-turn, and leverages our image as preflop aggressor. If re-raised/shoved, we have outs and implied odds deepstacked. Optimal play to maximize EV long-term.

Flop: A♣ 8♣ 3♣. I hold Q♠ T♣ — one club for a four‑flush (≈35% to hit by river vs a single opponent) but no pair. I bet 450 as a semi‑bluff and got raised to 1100, then Elon shoved it to 3300. Pot is 5750 and the call is 2500 (all‑in) — that gives me roughly 2500/(5750+2500) ≈ 30% break‑even equity. Facing two heavy raises (first Flash to 1100, then a huge raise from the very deep Elon) strongly polarizes their ranges toward made hands: sets, two‑pair/boats, or very likely made flushes (two clubs) — and if I do hit my flush it can easily be the second‑best. I'm out of position, have limited fold equity and vulnerable redraws, so my effective equity versus their combined raising range is almost certainly below the break‑even. I planned to fold to heavy resistance with only a draw. Preserve the 25BB stack for better spots rather than risking it in a low EV all‑in. Fold.

I have flopped a Jack-high flush (Jc 6c) on a monochrome board (Ac 8c 3c). This is a very strong made hand, though it is the 'third nuts' behind a King-high or Queen-high flush. In this hand, after Minni led the flop and I raised to 1100, Elon 3-bet to 3300. Elon has shown a high level of aggression in previous hands (Hands #17, #25, and #28), often using his large stack to apply maximum pressure. While his 3-bet on this texture is polarizing, I cannot fold a flush given the pot odds (2200 to call into a 5750 pot) and the stack-to-pot ratio. If I call the 2200, I will have 4600 chips remaining with a pot of nearly 8000. Given the depth, I am effectively committed to the hand. By shoving (raising all-in), I maximize value from his sets (88, 33) and top pairs with a high club (like Ac Kc or Ac Qc), while protecting my hand against a fourth club appearing on the turn. My total flop commitment will be my current 1100 plus my remaining stack of 6800, totaling 7900.

Facing a massive reraise from Flash to 7900 total (4600 to call) into a 12550 pot after I raised his flop raise on this monotone Ac8c3c board. My Ad7c gives top pair weak kicker + strong club flush draw (9 outs). Position is great on button, pot odds ~27% required (~3.7:1), and we have ~20% immediate equity vs a made flush (plus pair/set outs and implied odds if we improve). However, Flash's line is screaming monster: loose BB preflop call, then raise over Minni's weak 450 bet (repping strength), and now huge reraise over my 3300 isolation raise after Minni folds. His range is heavily weighted toward made flushes (any two clubs like KcQc, JcTc, AcXc), sets (88/33), or AcXc combos. Bluffing this aggressively OOP after my raise is unlikely given history (Flash value-bets strong hands like in H33, folds to pressure like H32). Shoving 24950 (~2.2x) has low fold equity vs nuts and leaves us committed behind. Calling risks 18% stack with reverse implied odds if behind (he'll bomb turn/river); folding preserves our deep 25k stack (250BB+) for better spots in early tournament (34/150 hands). Opponents exploitable later; no need to cooler ourselves here. Standard fold despite hand strength.

I’m on the button with K♦9♦ in a 3-handed game and a 2,500 stack (25 BB). This is a very playable hand in position — good high-card value plus suit and postflop playability. I want to seize the initiative, avoid limp-induced multiway pots against two deep, aggressive stacks, and use my positional advantage. A standard 3x BB open (300 total) gives meaningful fold equity against the SB, keeps the pot size manageable, and leaves me ~22 BB behind to play postflop. Min-raising to 200 is too small and will invite more callers; shoving is unnecessary with 25 BB. Plan: open-raise to 300 to steal or play postflop in position; re-evaluate if I face a large 3-bet (fold to overcommitting 3-bets, consider shove only if it commits most of my stack).

I am in the Small Blind with Jack-Ten suited, which is a very strong hand in a three-handed game. The Button (Minni) has raised to 300. Minni is relatively short-stacked with only about 2200 chips remaining (22 Big Blinds). While 3-betting to isolate is a strong option, calling is also viable to see a flop with a hand that has great playability and multi-way potential, especially if Elon (the Big Blind) also decides to enter the pot. Given the stack sizes and the 3-handed dynamic, I will call the additional 250 and see how the flop develops.

89h suited connectors from the big blind facing a button raise to 300 and SB call. Pot is 700, calling 200 more gives excellent pot odds of about 3.5:1, requiring only ~22% equity, which 89s achieves easily with playability, flush/straight potential, and strong implied odds given deep stacks (mine and Flash's). Minni is short-stacked at 2200 (~22BB), likely to act aggressively postflop, but calling keeps the pot multi-way cheaply OOP. Folding is too tight; raising risks isolation OOP against two players with position potential lost. Standard speculative call to realize equity.

I have flopped an open-ended straight draw with Jack-Ten of Spades on a King-Queen-Five board. As the first to act, checking to the pre-flop aggressor (Minni) is the standard play. Minni is short-stacked with only 2200 remaining, so a bet from her might signal significant strength or a commitment to the pot. Elon is also in the hand with a massive stack. By checking, I can evaluate the action behind me and decide whether to call or raise based on the bet sizing and the potential for a free card.

Flop Qh 5d Kh gives me 89hh a powerful nut flush draw with 9 outs (~35% equity vs typical calling range), backdoor straight possibilities, but no made hand. Pot 900, checked to me in BB after SB Flash's check (likely weak after flat preflop raise). Minni (button raiser, short ~2200) likely to c-bet wide; checking induces her bet, setting up ideal check-raise semi-bluff all-in or large raise, leveraging fold equity and massive implied odds from Flash's deep stack. If Minni checks (possible trap/value), free turn card on scary coordinated board. Donk betting OOP risks multiway isolation, gets raised/folded to, or called by worse draws; checking exploits opponents' passivity (history of check-calls), balances villainous image from preflop limp-call, and maximizes EV long-term in deep 3-handed play.

I have top pair (Kd) with a mediocre 9 kicker on K♠? (board K♥ Q♥ 5♦) in position after opening preflop. There are two opponents and the pot is 900; both checked the flop, so betting gives me initiative and a chance to take the pot now or get value from worse Ks, Qx, and 5x hands. I also want to deny equity to backdoor draws and charge any overcards that might draw out. A ~2/3-pot bet (~600) is a size that extracts value while not overcommitting my ~22BB stack. If I face a large check-raise I’ll re-evaluate and likely fold rather than commit with a medium kicker; but I expect many worse hands to call. Betting 600 keeps control of the hand and leverages my position.

I have flopped an open-ended straight draw (Jack-Ten on a King-Queen-Five board) which gives me 8 outs to a very strong hand. Minni, who was the pre-flop aggressor, has bet 600 into the 900 pre-flop pot, leaving herself with only 1600 chips. My pot odds are 600 to call for a total pot of 2100, which is approximately 28.5%. While the mathematical probability of hitting the straight on the turn is about 17%, the implied odds are high because Minni is likely committed to the pot with any top or middle pair, and I can potentially stack her if I hit. Additionally, Elon is still in the hand with a massive stack, which could further increase the pot's value if he also calls. Folding an open-ended straight draw for one bet here would be too tight.

Facing a 600 bet into 2100 after Flash's call, with 89hh holding the nut flush draw (9 outs, ~35-40% equity multiway) on this draw-heavy Qh5dKh board. Pot odds are excellent (600 to win 2700, ~18% required equity). Minni (button, short stack ~1600 behind) likely c-betting wide, Flash's SB call suggests medium strength/draw/weakness. Calling keeps the pot multiway OOP, maximizing implied odds from my deep stack (24k) and Flash's (11k) if I hit the nuts. Raising risks folding out Flash's equity-denying calls or isolating vs Minni's shove (potentially ahead but denies dead money); check-fold too tight pre-turn. Turn likely brings action anyway on scary board; call realizes equity passively while pot grows efficiently with villain ranges.

I have turned the nut straight (10-J-Q-K-A). There is a heart flush draw on the board, but my hand is currently unbeatable. Minni, the pre-flop aggressor, is very short-stacked with only 1600 chips remaining in a 2700 chip pot. If I check, I am highly likely to induce a shove from Minni, which may also encourage Elon (the big stack) to call or raise, allowing me to maximize the value of my hand. Leading out here might fold out Elon's weaker holdings, whereas checking allows the short stack to commit herself and potentially traps the bigger stack.

Turn board As is somewhat blankish but keeps flush draws live. Holding the nut flush draw (9h 8h for 9 clean outs to nuts ~18% hit river) with massive implied odds given my 24k stack vs Flash's 11k and Minni's short 1.6k. Flash's SB check after calling flop c-bet OOP suggests marginal strength/draw/weak pair, unwilling to bet into Minni. Checking OOP exploits this passivity, induces aggressive action from button Minni (history of postflop aggression when short, likely to bet/shove wide for value/protection), building pot cheaply if called/shoved while denying her free river card. Pot already 2700 (my investment ~800), check maintains pot control, balances prior passive check-call image, and sets up river check-raise/shove if I improve or villain bluffs. Betting now (~3/4 pot 2000) risks multiway isolation OOP, Flash folding equity-giving hands, or facing Minni shove (~even money bad if behind); checking maximizes EV long-term in 3-handed deep play by realizing equity passively. Fold equity from Minni higher post-check.

Board on the turn is Q♥ 5♦ K♥ A♠ and I hold K♦9♦ — top pair but a weak kicker. Pot is 2700 and I have 1600 (16BB) left. Both opponents called my flop bet and then checked the Ace on the turn; that looks like weakness but the Ace also creates many hands (Ax) that now beat me. Shoving or a large bet here has poor fold equity against two opponents — jamming 1600 into 2700 gives callers roughly 27% required equity, so they will call very wide (including hands that already beat me). Betting small just builds the pot and leaves me vulnerable to a check-raise or a caller with an Ace. With a vulnerable one-pair hand in a three-way pot I want pot control and to see the river in position. Plan: check the turn, re-evaluate on the river — extract value with a small bet if it checks through and I’m still likely ahead, call small river bets, and fold to clear, large aggression that represents an Ace or two pair.

I have the Broadway straight (Ten through Ace), which is the absolute nuts on this board. The turn checked through after Minni (the short stack) and Elon (the deep stack) both called on the flop. With only 1600 chips remaining in Minni's stack and a pot of 2700, I want to ensure I get paid. If I check again, I risk the hand checking down and losing potential value. By betting 1600, I effectively put Minni all-in and offer Elon a price to call with any Ace, King, or two-pair combination. If Elon raises, I have the nuts and can comfortably re-raise or call depending on his action to maximize value.

River 6c is a blank that misses my nut flush draw completely; I have 89hh for pure air with zero made hand, pair, or showdown value. Pot 4300, Flash (SB) leads for 1600 after passive check/call flop c-bet and check turn, into Minni short (~1600). Flash's line (SB limp/call pre wide, check/call 600 flop on draw-heavy KhQh5d, check turn As) is consistent with medium strength like weak Kx, Qx, pair+draw that holds up or semi-bluff/value on river. While Flash could be bluffing missed draws, calling 1600 requires ~21% equity (to win 5900), which air doesn't provide—far better to bluff catch with ace-high or weak pairs from passive image. Minni may call/shove her short stack, but I have no fold equity or reason to continue OOP with nothing. Folding preserves deep stack (24k) for better spots in 3-handed tournament; consistent with prior passive play but disciplined river decision.

Fold. I have top pair (K♦9♦) but a very weak kicker and calling Flash’s river raise (1,600) would commit my remaining 1,600 all‑in for a pot of 4,300 — I need ~37% equity to break even. Flash’s line (called pre and flop, checked the Ace on the turn, then raises the river) is exactly the line for value-heavy hands that beat me (many Ax combos, A5/Ax two‑pairs, sets, and some Kx with better kickers), not a spot with a high bluff frequency. With only 16 BB left this is a marginal, commit‑or‑bust decision; preserving my stack for a clearer +EV spot is the correct play. I’ll fold and wait for a better opportunity.

I am on the button with 8h 6c, a weak offsuit hand. The Big Blind (Minni) is short-stacked with only 15 BBs, making them highly likely to shove over a steal attempt. The Small Blind (Elon) is the chip leader and has shown a willingness to be active. Given the stack dynamics and my poor hand, folding is the most prudent play to avoid unnecessary chip loss.

Preflop, unopened pot after Button fold. Holding J6s suited connectors, a strong small blind raising hand with good playability, straight/flush potential, and fold equity against the short-stacked BB (15BB). Raising builds the pot, applies pressure to Minni who may fold weak hands or jam wide, against which J6s has decent equity (~35-40% vs typical jam range). Standard size 2.5x BB for value/steal. OOP postflop but deep stack allows flexibility. Limping too passive, better to take initiative.

I’m in the big blind with A♦T (AhTd) and 15BB effective (stack 1500). Elon (SB) opened to 250, pot is 350 and I must call 150 to see a flop. With this short stack I don’t want to limp/call and play a shallow, out-of-position postflop war — that’s low EV. ATo is a shove-worthy holding here: it blocks many A‑combos, has decent equity vs a typical SB calling range, and gives meaningful fold equity against Elon’s open. A small 3-bet to 400 would leave me with awkward postflop SPR; calling is too passive. Shoving maximizes immediate fold equity and, if called, still has acceptable showdown equity. I will shove my stack — total commit 1500 chips (all-in).

Facing a 3-bet shove from a short-stacked Minni (~16BB effective before her action, now 1BB left) in the BB after my SB open-raise. J6hh is a highly playable suited connector with excellent equity (~40-45%) against her wide jamming range, which history shows is aggressive (frequent 3-bets/shoves with speculative hands). Pot odds are ~2.4:1 (1250 to call into 3000 pot), requiring ~42% equity, which we meet or exceed. Risk is minimal relative to my deep 238BB stack (1250 is ~5%).
